title: pcr_read returns BufferTooSmall for wrong digest length
description: In wolftpm/src/device.rs, pcr_read() returns Err(Error::BufferTooSmall) when digest_len \!= 32 (line 122-124). The buffer (64 bytes) was not too small — the TPM returned an unexpected output size. Using BufferTooSmall here is misleading; a reader would expect this error to mean the caller's buffer was undersized. A dedicated variant (e.g. UnexpectedResponseSize) or InvalidArg would communicate the actual problem. At minimum a doc comment on the return path should explain why BufferTooSmall is reused here.
